observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide legal basis for the payment of OT services to the Assistant Municipal Accountant, otherwise, cause the refund of the amount paid.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed to observed the provisions of CSC and DBM Joint Circular No. 1 dated November 25, 2015 on payment of OT services.
observations.reason: No document was submitted. Likewise no refund has been made by the concerned official.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: Documents were still not submitted nor refund has been made to settle suspensions/ disallowances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (i) initiate the conduct of reconciliation of the Cash- in- Bank records
with the depository bank within ten (10) days from receipt of the Bank Statements
and with the Treasurer’s cash records at least quarterly; (ii) religiously prepare
monthly bank reconciliation statements for all accounts maintained by the
Municipality; and (iii) effect the reversion of the unreleased checks at year-end basis
to ensure the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (i) observe prudence in supervising the functions of her office and
ensure the integrity of recording the day-to-day financial events of the Municipality
in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules; and (ii) draw adjusting
entries to effect the corrections of accounting errors through the following proposed
journal entries:

Nature of Error Account Name Dr Cr
Double Set-up of November 1-30, Due from Officers and Employees ₱ 200.00
Salaries Due to Officers and Employees 1,681,976.61
Due to BIR 249,787.00
Due to GSIS 1,059,810.14
Due to Pag-IBIG 70,112.95
Due to PhilHealth 90,266.13
Due to GOCCs 550,155.49
Other Payables 179,100.00
Prior Year’s Expenses 72,396.00
Due to Other Funds ₱ 70,400.00
Prior Year’s Expenses 3,883,404.32
Total ₱ 3,953,804.32 ₱ 3,953,804.32
Check No. 660909 for deposit to Due to Officers and Employees ₱ 681,236.13
ATM Payroll Due to GSIS ₱ 681,236.13
Check No. 660910 for deposit to Due to Officers and Employees ₱ 174,982.18
ATM Payroll Due to GSIS ₱ 174,982.18
Check No. 660951 for deposit to Due to Officers and Employees ₱ 698,924.38
ATM Payroll Due to GSIS ₱ 698,924.38
Check No. 660953 for deposit to Due to Officers and Employees ₱ 171,382.00
ATM Payroll Due to GSIS ₱ 171,382.00
Check No. 660958 for remittance of Due to GOCCs ₱ 471,705.04
loan repayments Due to GSIS ₱ 471,705.04

We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Inventory Team
to (i) undertake complete physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of
the municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of
PPE and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period;
(b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards; (c)
require the Acting-General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain property cards for
PPE to account for the receipt and disposition of properties; and (d) require the
Acting-GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their property records after the
conduct of physical count to establish correctness of balances of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to draw an adjusting entry recognizing the capitalizable borrowing costs
through the following journal entry:

CIP-Buildings and Other Structures ₱124,819.71
Prior Period Adjustment ₱124,819.71
Henceforth, ensure to capitalize the interest expense that are directly attributable to
the acquisition, construction or production of a qualifying asset as part of the cost of
that asset.

We recommended and the Municipal Development Council headed by the
Local Chief Executive agreed that (a) Municipal Planning and Development Council
should closely monitor/supervise the project implementation to ensure completion
within the time frame; (b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should strictly adhere
with the procurement process and procedures as prescribed under Revised IRR of
R.A. 9184; and (c) Municipal Accountant should immediately submit the unsubmitted
DVs cited above and strategize prudently to avoid delays in the submission of
transaction documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) LDRRM
Officer to cause the submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
DRRMF to COA Auditor and DILG-LGOO as required by COA Circular No. 2012-
002; (b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the excess NDRRMF to the Bureau
of Treasury; and (c) Municipal Accountant to expedite the submission of DVs
including the required documentary requirements.

We recommended and the (a) Local School Board agreed to (i) carefully plan
the appropriation of funds to address the needs for the operation and maintenance of
public schools; and (ii) issue a supplemental budget for re-alignment to expenditures
not included in the annual appropriation; and (b) Municipal Accountant agreed to
ensure the completeness of the supporting documents in accordance to DepEd, DBM,
and DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
(i) issue Executive Order for the reconstitution/creation of the GFPS including its
ExeCOM and TWG relevant for gender mainstreaming; (ii) instruct the newly
reconstituted/created GFPS to create a centralized GAD database that would
consolidate the information gathered and compiled by all offices to include gender
statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data, as basis in GAD planning,
programming and policy making; and (iii) employ the Harmonized Gender and
Development Guidelines (HGDG) in the attribution of costs of regular
program/project to GAD and (iv) comply on the timely submission to the Audit Team
of the GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (i) convene all responsible
heads of offices and admonish them of their duties and functions on the submission
of the accounts, financial records and reports as sanctioned by COA; and (ii) instruct
the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (i) immediately submit all
transactions documents including the disbursement vouchers, and their supporting
documents and (ii) henceforth, observe the timeliness of reportorial requirements of
the Commission.

We recommended that the Management adopt and issue policies/guidelines
specifically designating the parameters, extent, and coverage of the AICS program.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Engineer to closely monitor the installation of signboards/tarpaulins detailing the
complete and relevant information of Programs Projects Activities (PPAs)
implemented by the Municipality, and document the compliance of the contractor for
the said work item.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) the Local
Finance Committee to prepare the required reports and observe prudence on the
disclosure policy prescribed by the guidelines; and (b) revert the unutilized funds to
National Treasury.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Human Resource
Management Office to submit regularly the advance copies of Contracts and
Purchase Orders together with supporting documents to the Auditor within the
prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to revisit the prior year’s transactions and conduct the reconciliation of
the balances of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and effect
the necessary adjustments as warranted to bring their balances into agreement.
Henceforth, exercise due care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s
financial transactions to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are equal at
all times.

We commended the management for its continuous efforts to comply with the
COVID-19 vaccination program guidelines, a way of providing protection to their
constituents from adverse effects of the virus. We also recommended that the
management continue to administer vaccines efficiently so that local herd immunity
or 70% of the target population can be attained in the soonest time possible.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant (a) to prepare complete and accurate statement of account for remittance
of taxes/premiums/loans repayments deducted from suppliers/contractors/employees
to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth; (b) exert extra effort in identifying the
nature of negative/unremitted prior year’s balance of Due to GSIS and Due to Pag-
IBIG and make the necessary reconciliation thereof to facilitate correction to bring
the account balances accurate; and (c) update regularly the posting of transactions in
the Subsidiary Ledgers to ensure correctness of account balance to ensure the
accuracy of the reported balances.

We recommended and management agreed to exercise due prudence in the
disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.

observations.managementAction: The LGU  already created the Inventory Committee pursuant to Executive Order No. 10 s. 2021
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Observation No. 3











Lack of personnel, heavy workload of the GSO and Accountant and infliction to Covid-19 cause the non-implementation of the inventory taking for CY 2021.


Most of the staff are JO personnel who are changing frequently.


Lack of awareness on the required records that should be maintained by General Services Office.
Non-conduct of inventory taking and incomplete records by both Accounting and Property Department

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the 

(a) Municipal Accountant to 

(i) submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements for the months of January-December 2020 for all the banks accounts maintained by the Municipality; 

(ii) strictly observe the timely submission of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements; and 


(iii) analyze the transactions recorded in the Subsidiary Ledgers to determine the erroneous entry made which resulted to a discrepancy in the balances in the controlling accounts, the General Ledgers; and 

(b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to 

(i) conduct periodic reconciliation of all Cash in Bank accounts to ascertain the accuracy of the balances of accounts appearing in the Cashbooks and Accounting Financial Records; and 

(ii) investigate the differences and adjust the corresponding ledgers/cashbooks accordingly.
observations.managementAction: BRS for the period covering January to December 2020 were already submitted to COA.
observations.reason:
Delayed submission and non-submission of other bank accounts maintained by the Municipality was still noted in audit.

Locating the records of the reconciling items is still on-going.



Lack of coordination between Accounting and Treasury Department.


Reconciliation of records has not yet been made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) identify the problems concerning the failure to submit the transaction documents/Disbursement Vouchers and determine and implement the best possible measures for the prompt submission thereof; and

(b) submit the required documents/DVs pursuant to Sections 100 and 122 of PD No. 1445.

We also recommended the withholding of salary of persons responsible for the continued failure to submit the transaction documents/Disbursement Vouchers until the submission of all the required accounts/documents for audit.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive gave instructions to the concerned official to facilitate the implementa-tion of audit recommenda-tions.
observations.reason:
Some transaction documents were still not submitted.


No action taken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly trial balances and year-end financial statements for all funds as required under Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 and Section 70 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I as amended by Section 361 of Government Accounting Manual, Volume I, for LGUs.

Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive make a personal re-assessment and evaluation on the performance of the Accounting Unit and accordingly introduce necessary corrective measures pursuant to penalty clause under Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.
observations.managementAction: The year-end Financial Statements was submitted on February 14, 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all subsequent financial reports are submitted within the reglementary period as prescribed in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salary of persons responsible for failing to submit the financial reports until the submission of all the required reports for audit.
observations.managementAction: The deadline for the submission of monthly Trial Balance and Quarterly Financial Statements was fully observed by the Municipal Accountant.
The concerned officials already implemented the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to 

(a) require the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO) to 

(i) take necessary action to prove the receipt of five beneficiaries that cannot be located during the conduct of 
random confirmation; 

(ii) facilitate the certification of identification cards submitted by the beneficiaries; and 

(iii) ensure compliance with the guidelines on emergency program pursuant to DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 04 s. 2020; and  

(b) MSWDO, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to 

(i) cause the submission of photos of the beneficiaries during the actual receipt of the assistance and arrange the submitted identification cards in chronological order based on the name of beneficiaries as appearing in the payrolls.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO had already take appropriate actions to implement the recommenda-tions of COA.

The lacking documents have already submitted by the MDDRMO and Municipal Accountant. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant recognized the inventory in the books using the perpetual method. She also ensures that DVs are  supported with complete documenta-tion and properly accomplished.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and BAC Chairman to submit the required documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor for proper/further evaluation of the transactions pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and comply with the required reportorial requirements of GPPB Circular 2020-001; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant 

(i) ensure that all the supporting documents are properly signed and the necessary information in various forms are properly and completely filled-up; and 

(ii) explain/justify the discrepancies noted in the supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Instructions were given to the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommenda-tions.
observations.reason:
Some documents were still not submitted.








No justification was submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) ensure that the GAD Focal Point System are in place and fully functional; and 

(b) require the GAD Focal Person to 

(i) maintain a GAD database that could either be manually operated or developed through software; 

(ii) propose a training program on the HGDG in its GAD Plan and Budget for the incoming year and seek the assistance of a gender analysis specialist/expert in the use of and/or administration of the HGDG; 

(iii) deepen their knowledge and build their competencies on GAD-related laws and international commitments, gender mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting and GAD-related tools among others; and 

(iv) ensure that GAD Plan and Budget be fully implemented when the current situation is back to normal.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Observation No. 8











The reconstitution of the GFPS is not yet undertaken.



Lack of manpower resources hindered the completion of GAD Database.


Due to pandemic and health protocols hindered the conduct trainings on GAD.




-do-




-do-

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) refrain from approving/allowing payment of claims against the government funds without complete supporting documents pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445; 

(b) direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of the supporting documents before certifying box A of the Disbursement Vouchers; and 

(c) require the concerned Municipal Officials to submit the lacking supporting documents to properly support the validity and propriety of transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed the concerned official to comply with the audit recommenda-tions.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were still not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to 

(a) refrain from charging prior year’s expenditures against the current year appropriations; 

(b) obligate/recognize the expenditures in the year they are incurred to reflect the correctness of accounts recorded in the books; and 

(c) adhere to the provisions of Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 119 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 in the use of appropriations to prevent the incurrence of irregular expenditures and to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer ensures that all expenditures are obligated in the year they are incurred.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require all accountable officers to prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits on a daily basis to facilitate the intact remittance/deposits of collections to its depository bank pursuant to Section 63 of the Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Accountable Officers are now preparing the RCDs on a daily basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the designated officer responsible for the receipt, distribution and inventory of donations in-kind to submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and supporting documents of the donations in-kind received from all  the donors such as the acknowledgement receipt, proof of distribution and inventory report as required under Section II.1.a of COA Circular 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed the Municipal Accountant and the Acting GSO to fully documented the receipt, distribution and inventory of donations in-kind.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local School Board

(a) conduct proper review/evaluation on programs/projects/activities to ensure that only eligible expenses shall be charged to Special Education Fund (SEF) in compliance with the Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and 

(b) discontinue charging to SEF the medical assistance granted to DepEd teachers/utility personnel.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive refrained the charging of ineligible expenses to the Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman, Human Resources and Management Officer and  General Services Officer/ICO-Municipal Treasurer to: 

(i) strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase/Job Orders/Contracts and its supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection; and 

(ii) furnish the Office of the Auditor copy of Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively, on a sampling basis.
observations.managementAction: The submission of POs for goods is now being observed by GSO.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Observation No. 13






No action was taken by HRMO and BAC to implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive

(a) instruct the Collecting Officers/Accountable Officers to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) to the Municipal Treasurer; and 

(b) require the Municipal Treasurer to consolidate the individual RAAF and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month for the timely monitoring of accountabilities on various accountable forms in compliance with Section 51 of the Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units, Volume II.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer and Accountable Officers have already submitted the monthly RAAF for CY 
2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to 

(i) submit to Office of the Auditor all the aforesaid cancelled checks for inspection; and 

(ii) henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the Report of Checks Issued and reported chronologically with the other checks issued with the word Cancelled clearly indicated in the face of the check.
observations.managementAction: The cancelled checks are now attached to the Report of Issued submitted to Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit all the taxes withheld within ten (10) days following the end of the month in compliance with the Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality ensures that rules and regulations on withholding and remittances taxes withheld from employees and suppliers /  contractors are being complied.
observations.reason: The intact remittance of taxes withheld was not fully observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit intact all compulsory contributions/loan repayments withheld to the GSIS and adhere the requirements of Republic Act No. 8291.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality ensures that rules and regulations on withholding and remittances of mandatory contributions/deductions for GSIS are being complied.
observations.reason: The intact remittance of contributions/loans repayments was not fully observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly all compulsory contributions/amortization withheld within the reglementary period and observe the full or intact remittance thereof to the Pag-IBIG.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality ensures that rules and regulations on withholding and remittances of mandatory contributions/deductions for Pag-IBIG are being complied.
observations.reason: The intact remittance of contributions/loans repayments was not fully observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the PhilHealth.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality ensures that rules and regulations on withholding and remittances of mandatory contributions/deductions for PhilHealth are being complied.
observations.reason: The intact remittance of contributions was not fully observed.
